summary: Handle server command responses without "ok" field
description: According to SERVER-10891, commands don't return "ok" field if targeting databases with invalid characters. (Essentially, the server is failing to execute a query on "$cmd", and it hasn't yet entered the command-processing path.) Drivers must expect a command response document without an "ok" field, in which case "$err" will be set.

After looking into this more closely, it appears that it _kinda_ works on windows.  

After editing the mongo-orchestration.config file, you can start the restful server using {code}python server.py --no-fork{code}.  Using this without --no-fork causes some issues related to spinning up a separate process and I haven't worked through that yet.

Once this is up, you can run {code}python helpers\cmd_hosts.py{code}.  This will put you into a little repl in which you can do a couple of things.
# create {"name": "mongod"} will appear to fail, but really do what it is supposed to do
# info 1 will provide back the information that should have come back from the create call
# start 1 will start the guy you created
# stop 1 will appear to fail, but does actually stop the server

I haven't done anything with replica sets or shards.

summary: drivers should SO_LINGER when closing sockets
description:
suppose my program does some writes and immediately then closes the connection and terminates.  do my writes get there?

generally they will, if SO_LINGER is specified - but see article below for caveats.  so

1) should we use SO_LINGER in all drivers? i think so?
2) in addition, if last operation on a connection was a write, should we automatically call getlasterror on connection destruction?

re:#2, even if the data gets there, the GLE call can be argued for as my expectation might be that my program does not terminate until the data is delivered and applied.

* http://blog.netherlabs.nl/articles/2009/01/18/the-ultimate-so_linger-page-or-why-is-my-tcp-not-reliable

summary: gridfs chunksize should be lowered to 255K
description: Drivers should lower chunk size to 255K (from 256K).

summary: Implement default write acknowledgement with new MongoClient class
drivers should use $query and $orderby in query spec instead of query and orderby
Commands sent to mongos sometimes break when sending $readPreference
description:
This ticket is meant to work around the issues described in SERVER-2474, SERVER-7768, SERVER-7789, etc.

As of the MongoDB 2.2-era driver releases, drivers send a $readPreference subdocument with some commands to mongos. Pre-2.4 versions of mongos throw an error when they receive this unexpected field.

Drivers should follow this algorithm:

If read preference is PRIMARY, don't send $readPreference to mongos. The default is PRIMARY so sending $readPreference is redundant anyway. If the ReadPreference is SecondaryPreferred *and no tags have been specified* do not send $readPreference. If read preference is anything other than PRIMARY always set the slaveOk bit.

summary: Drivers should check the query failure bit even on getmore response
description:
The query failure bit (bit 1 in the responseFlags field of the server's reply message - see link below) should be checked for replies to OP_GET_MORE.  See SERVER-9738 for a related server issue.

http://docs.mongodb.org/meta-driver/latest/legacy/mongodb-wire-protocol/#op-reply

summary: Drivers should only talk to servers with overlapping wire versions
description:
Drivers must assume a value of 0 for both minWireVersion and maxWireVersion if those fields are missing from the isMaster response, in order to preserve compatibility with servers prior to 2.5.2.

Additionally, each version of a driver must have its own min and max wire version, representing the range of servers that it is compatible with.
